title: Stephanotrichum octonum
taxonomy: Stephanotrichum octonum is a species of fungus in the family Pterulaceae. It belongs to the phylum Basidiomycota, which comprises a large group of fungi that reproduce via spores. Stephanotrichum octonum can be further classified as a member of the class Agaricomycetes, a group of fungi characterized by their club-shaped spores. The order Pleurotiales and the family Pterulaceae are also part of its classification. Stephanotrichum octonum's classification is an example of how scientists use a hierarchical system to categorize and understand the diversity of fungi.

intro: Stephanotrichum octonum is a plant species, commonly known as a species of fungus, that belongs to the family Parmeliaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, where it is believed to possess health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Stephanotrichum octonum include compounds like usnic acid, which is known for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Stephanotrichum octonum is often prepared in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
